findings.evidence: `Quotient.lean:125-127` states `lemma orbitQuotientToBase_surjective (hreg : IsRegular p)` and proves it by `Quotient.lift_surjective p _ hreg.1`; Mathlib provides `Quotient.lift_surjective` for any surjective representative map (`mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Quot.lean:312`).
findings.fix: Move/generalize this lemma outside `IsRegular` with signature `lemma orbitQuotientToBase_surjective (hp : Function.Surjective p) : Function.Surjective (orbitQuotientToBase p)`, and use it from `orbitQuotientEquivBase`.
findings.issue: `IsRegular.orbitQuotientToBase_surjective` requires `hreg : IsRegular p`, but its proof uses only `hreg.1 : Function.Surjective p`; fibre transitivity is irrelevant to surjectivity of the quotient lift.

summary: The quotient/base equivalence is now stated at quotient level, but one exposed API lemma keeps an unnecessary regularity hypothesis where plain surjectivity of the original map is enough.
